sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see the block is at:
- Orange (node 7): rows 30-31, cols 34-38
- Blue (node 10): rows 32-34, cols 34-38
The corridor is at cols 29-33 (vertical). The block is at cols 34-38, which is to the right of the corridor. So the block is still NOT in the corridor - it's adjacent to it.
Wait, looking at row 30: GGGGGGGGGGGGGGGcccccOOOOOGGGGGG
The ccccc is at cols 29-33, and OOOOO is at cols 34-38. So the block is still to the right of the corridor.
The LEFT action moved the block from cols 34-38 to... still cols 34-38? No wait, let me recheck. Before the UP+LEFT, the block was at rows 35-39, cols 34-38. After UP+LEFT, it's at rows 30-34, cols 34-38. So UP moved it up by 5 rows (from 35 to 30), but LEFT didn't move it left at all!
So LEFT still doesn't work. The block can move up and down but not left from this position. Maybe the corridor is not the path - maybe the block needs to go somewhere else.
Let me reconsider the goal. Looking at the room:
- The white cross is at rows 31-33, cols 19-22 (left side)
- The dark gray corridor goes vertically at cols 29-33 and horizontally at rows 40-44, cols 14-33
- The block is at rows 30-34, cols 34-38
Maybe the goal is not to go through the corridor but to match some pattern. Let me look at the blue shapes in the black frames:
1. Top frame (nodes 4, 5): small blue L-shape
2. Bottom-left frame (nodes 13, 14): larger blue L-shape
These might be templates showing where the block needs to go. Or maybe they're just decorative.
Let me try a different approach. Maybe the goal is to move the block to a position where it "completes" the dark gray corridor pattern, or maybe to overlap with the white cross.
Actually, let me reconsider. The block can move UP and DOWN freely, and RIGHT and LEFT work too (we tested RIGHT earlier and it moved). But LEFT from the corridor area doesn't work. Maybe there's a wall blocking LEFT movement?
Let me try moving the block DOWN first, then LEFT, to see if it can enter the horizontal corridor.
